Pathway_Genes.Variety: M82 for species survey; MicroTom for tomato VIGS/transient assays; Arabidopsis Col-0 used for heterologous overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: 1. Recombinant protein enzyme assay using purified protein with quercetin and UDP-glucose/UDP-galactose followed by LC-MS. 2. Heterologous overexpression in Arabidopsis thaliana (Col-0 and f3gt mutant background) with metabolite profiling. 3. Tomato fruit VIGS suppression in MicroTom with LC-MS metabolite profiling. 4. Transient overexpression in tomato fruit with LC-MS metabolite profiling.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase; UGT78D subfamily; PSPG motif-containing glycosyltransferase
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream decoration step after flavonol aglycone formation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Flavonol-3-O-glucosides (e.g., quercetin 3-O-glucoside and related flavonol-3Glc compounds)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to the 3-O position of flavonoid/flavonol aglycones to generate flavonol-3-O-glucosides
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms direct glycosylated products and supplies immediate precursors for further decorated flavonol glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Polyphenolic antioxidants with reported health-beneficial properties; also implicated in plant tolerance to UV, cold, drought, diseases, and pests
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruit peel/pericarp, leaves, stems; tissue and species dependent across tomato accessions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of flavonol-3-O-glucosides and downstream flavonol glycosides; silencing lowers flavonol-3-O-glucosides whereas overexpression increases them
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for flavonol decoration; not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Predominantly associated with flavonol-accumulating tissues and species; sequenced orthologs from LYCO, PIMP, NEOR, HABR, and PENN showed conserved PSPG sites
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit ripening stage comparisons; agroinfiltration-based VIGS/transient expression in tomato fruit; heterologous expression in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter for Arabidopsis overexpression; endogenous promoter NA
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS metabolomics; GC-MS primary metabolomics; TOM2 microarray transcriptomics; public RNA-seq from Koenig et al. 2013 and Bolger et al. 2014; accession numbers NA in text
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Candidate target for metabolomics-assisted breeding, biofortification, and metabolic engineering to enhance tomato fruit polyphenolics/flavonols
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also referred to as SlUGT78D-a or SlUGT78D1-a and previously annotated as anthocyanin 3-O-glucosyltransferase in purple transgenic tomato analysis. Functional conservation inferred across multiple tomato species by conserved PSPG sequence.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Solyc10g083440 was identified as the principal candidate flavonoid 3-O-glucosyltransferase in tomato. 2. Recombinant protein catalyzed flavonoid 3-O-glucosylation in vitro. 3. Arabidopsis overexpressing this gene accumulated more flavonol-3Glc-related compounds than controls. 4. Tomato VIGS lines showed reduced flavonol-3-O-glucosides, while transient overexpression increased these metabolites. 5. The gene therefore causally promotes flavonol glycoside biosynthesis in tomato polyphenol metabolism.

Pathway_Genes.Variety: MicroTom for tomato VIGS/transient assays; expression inference across diverse tomato accessions including HABR and PENN
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Tomato fruit transient overexpression and VIGS suppression followed by LC-MS metabolite profiling; phylogenetic analysis and in silico expression analysis used as supporting evidence
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase; described as SlUGT73B-a candidate; PSPG motif-containing glycosyltransferase
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream decoration step acting on pre-glycosylated flavonol scaffolds
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Flavonoid/flavonol 7-O-glucosides and higher-order glycosides such as Q3Glc6''Rha7Glc-related products
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Putatively transfers glucose to the 7-O position of flavonoids/flavonols, generating 7-O-glucosylated derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Polyphenolic compounds associated with nutritional quality and likely stress-protective roles typical of flavonoids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves and fruits in a species-dependent manner; flavonol-7-O-glucosides absent in PENN according to metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate positive regulator/enzyme for accumulation of flavonol-7-O-glucosylated products, but evidence remains incomplete; transient overexpression caused relatively low Q3Glc6''Rha7Glc level and authors state further characterization is required
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key downstream decoration candidate; not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Predicted to be absent or non-expressed in PENN based on lack of flavonol-7-O-glucosides; candidate expression supported in HABR seedlings/leaves by in silico data
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit ripening stage comparisons; tomato fruit agroinfiltration for VIGS/transient expression
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS metabolomics; TOM2 microarray transcriptomics; public RNA-seq from Koenig et al. 2013 and Bolger et al. 2014; accession numbers NA in text
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Potential target to diversify tomato flavonol glycoside composition in breeding or engineering, but function requires stronger validation before deployment
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Phylogenetically, this gene did not cluster in a distinct F7GT clade, and the paper notes its sequence is not similar to proteins in other F7GT clades; therefore catalytic assignment remains tentative.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Solyc10g079350 was proposed as a tomato flavonoid 7-O-glucosyltransferase candidate based on metabolite presence/absence, expression patterns, and phylogeny. 2. Lack of flavonol-7-O-glucosides in S. pennellii suggested absence or non-expression of the corresponding activity. 3. Transient overexpression in tomato affected a 7-O-glucosylated flavonol product only weakly. 4. Authors concluded that further biochemical characterization is required, so this gene remains a candidate rather than conclusively validated enzyme.

Title: Exploiting Natural Variation in Tomato to Define Pathway Structure and Metabolic Regulation of Fruit Polyphenolics in the Lycopersicum Complex
